# xsession

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `xsession`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `xsession.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable X Session. |
| `xsession.initExtra` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra shell commands to run during initialization. |
| `xsession.numlock.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Num Lock. |
| `xsession.pointerCursor.defaultCursor` | `submodule` | Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.x11.defaultCursor`. |
| `xsession.pointerCursor.name` | `submodule` | Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.name`. |
| `xsession.pointerCursor.package` | `submodule` | Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.package`. |
| `xsession.pointerCursor.size` | `submodule` | Alias of {option}`home.pointerCursor.size`. |
| `xsession.preferStatusNotifierItems` | `boolean` | Whether tray applets should prefer using the Status Notifier Items (SNI) protocol, commonly called App Indicators. Note, not all tray applets or status bars support SNI. |
| `xsession.profileExtra` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra shell commands to run before session start. |
| `xsession.profilePath` | `string` | Path, relative to {env}`HOME`, where Home Manager should write the X profile script. |
| `xsession.scriptPath` | `string` | Path, relative to {env}`HOME`, where Home Manager should write the X session script. |
| `xsession.windowManager.awesome.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Awesome window manager. |
| `xsession.windowManager.awesome.luaModules` | `list of package` | List of lua packages available for being used in the Awesome configuration. |
| `xsession.windowManager.awesome.noArgb` | `boolean` | Disable client transparency support, which can be greatly detrimental to performance in some setups |
| `xsession.windowManager.awesome.package` | `package` | The awesome package to use. to use for running the Awesome WM |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.alwaysResetDesktops` | `boolean` | If set to `true`, desktops configured in {option}`monitors` will be reset every time the config is run. If set to `false`, desktops will only be configured the first time the config is run. This is useful if you want to dynamically add desktops and you don't want them to be destroyed if you re-run `bspwmrc`. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable bspwm window manager. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional shell commands to be run at the end of the config file. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.extraConfigEarly` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Like extraConfig, except commands are run at the start of the config file. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.monitors` | `attribute set of list of string` | Specifies the names of desktops to create on each monitor. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.package` | `package` | The bspwm package to use. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules` | `attribute set of (open submodule of attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string))` | Rule configuration. The keys of the attribute set are the targets of the rules. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.border` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node should have border. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.center` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node will be put in the center, in floating mode. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.desktop` | `null or string` | The desktop where the rule should be applied. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.focus` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node should gain focus on creation. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.follow` | `null or boolean` | Whether focus should follow the node when it is moved. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.hidden` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node should occupy any space. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.layer` | `null or one of "below", "normal", "above"` | The layer where a new window should spawn. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.locked` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node should ignore {command}`node --close` messages. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.manage` | `null or boolean` | Whether the window should be managed by bspwm. If false, the window will be ignored by bspwm entirely. This is useful for overlay apps, e.g. screenshot tools. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.marked` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node will be marked for deferred actions. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.monitor` | `null or string` | The monitor where the rule should be applied. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.node` | `null or string` | The node where the rule should be applied. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.private` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node should stay in the same tiling position and size. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.rectangle` | `null or string` | The node's geometry, in the format `WxH+X+Y`. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.splitDir` | `null or one of "north", "west", "south", "east"` | The direction where the container is going to be split. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.splitRatio` | `null or floating point number` | The ratio between the new window and the previous existing window in the desktop. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.state` | `null or one of "tiled", "pseudo_tiled", "floating", "fullscreen"` | The state in which a new window should spawn. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.rules.<name>.sticky` | `null or boolean` | Whether the node should stay on the focused desktop. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.settings` | `attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string or list of (boolean or signed integer or floating point number or string))` | General settings given to `bspc config`. |
| `xsession.windowManager.bspwm.startupPrograms` | `list of string` | Programs to be executed during startup. |
| `xsession.windowManager.command` | `string` | Command to use to start the window manager. The default value allows integration with NixOS' generated xserver configuration. Extra actions and commands can be specified in {option}`xsession.initExtra`. |
| `xsession.windowManager.exwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable exwm. |
| `xsession.windowManager.exwm.extraPackages` | `function that evaluates to a(n) list of package` | Extra packages available to Emacs. The value must be a function which receives the attrset defined in {var}`emacs.pkgs` as the sole argument. |
| `xsession.windowManager.exwm.loadScript` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Emacs lisp code to be run after loading the user's init file. |
| `xsession.windowManager.exwm.package` | `package` | The emacs package to use. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.apps` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Apps configuration for Fluxbox, written to {file}`~/.fluxbox/apps`. Look at the {manpage}`fluxbox(1)` manpage for details. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Fluxbox window manager. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.extraCommandLineArgs` | `list of string` | Extra command line arguments to pass to {command}`fluxbox`. Look at the {manpage}`fluxbox(1)` manpage for details. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.init` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Init configuration for Fluxbox, written to {file}`~/.fluxbox/init`. Look at the {manpage}`fluxbox(1)` manpage for details. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.keys` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Keyboard shortcuts configuration for Fluxbox, written to {file}`~/.fluxbox/keys`. Look at the {manpage}`fluxbox-keys(1)` manpage for details. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.menu` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Menu configuration for Fluxbox, written to {file}`~/.fluxbox/menu`. Look at the {manpage}`fluxbox-menu(1)` manpage for details. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.package` | `null or package` | The fluxbox package to use. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.slitlist` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Slitlist configuration for Fluxbox, written to {file}`~/.fluxbox/slitlist`. Look at the {manpage}`fluxbox(1)` manpage for details. |
| `xsession.windowManager.fluxbox.windowmenu` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Window menu configuration for Fluxbox, written to {file}`~/.fluxbox/windowmenu`. Look at the {manpage}`fluxbox-menu(1)` manpage for details. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable herbstluftwm window manager. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.enableAlias` | `boolean` | Set an alias for the {command}`herbstclient` command in the {file}`autostart` script that only stores its arguments and executes them all at once at the end of the {file}`autostart` script. This reduces the amount of flickering you get while all options are being applied and improves the performance. On the other hand, this makes it more difficult to write bash functions that call {command}`herbstclient`. You can work around this by calling {command}`command herbstclient` in your functions to still get some of the benefits of enabling this alias. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration lines to add verbatim to {file}`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/herbstluftwm/autostart`. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.keybinds` | `attribute set of string` | Herbstluftwm keybinds. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.mousebinds` | `attribute set of string` | Herbstluftwm mousebinds. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.package` | `package` | The herbstluftwm package to use. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.rules` | `list of string` | Herbstluftwm rules. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.settings` | `attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string)` | Herbstluftwm settings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.herbstluftwm.tags` | `list of string` | Tags to create on startup. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config` | `null or (submodule)` | i3 configuration options. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.assigns` | `attribute set of list of attribute set of (string or boolean)` | An attribute set that assigns applications to workspaces based on criteria. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars` | `list of (submodule)` | i3 bars settings blocks. Set to empty list to remove bars completely. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors` | `submodule` | Bar color settings. All color classes can be specified using submodules with 'border', 'background', 'text', fields and RGB color hex-codes as values. See default values for the reference. Note that 'background', 'status', and 'separator' parameters take a single RGB value. See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_colors>. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.activeWorkspace` | `null or (submodule)` | Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace is active. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.background` | `null or string` | Background color of the bar. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.bindingMode` | `null or (submodule)` | Border, background and text color for the binding mode indicator |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedBackground` | `null or string` | Background color of the bar on the currently focused monitor output. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedSeparator` | `null or string` | Text color to be used for the separator on the currently focused monitor output. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedStatusline` | `null or string` | Text color to be used for the statusline on the currently focused monitor output. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.focusedWorkspace` | `null or (submodule)` | Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace has focus. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.inactiveWorkspace` | `null or (submodule)` | Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace does not have focus and is not active. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.separator` | `null or string` | Text color to be used for the separator. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.statusline` | `null or string` | Text color to be used for the statusline. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.colors.urgentWorkspace` | `null or (submodule)` | Border, background and text color for a workspace button when the workspace contains a window with the urgency hint set. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.command` | `string` | Command that will be used to start a bar. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration lines for this bar. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.fonts` | `(list of string) or (submodule)` | Font configuration for this bar. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.hiddenState` | `null or one of "hide", "show"` | The default bar mode when 'bar.mode' == 'hide'. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.id` | `null or string` | Specifies the bar ID for the configured bar instance. If this option is missing, the ID is set to bar-x, where x corresponds to the position of the embedding bar block in the config file. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.mode` | `null or one of "dock", "hide", "invisible"` | Bar visibility mode. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.position` | `null or one of "top", "bottom"` | The edge of the screen i3bar should show up. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.statusCommand` | `null or string` | Command that will be used to get status lines. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.trayOutput` | `null or string` | Where to output tray. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.trayPadding` | `null or signed integer` | Sets the pixel padding of the system tray. This padding will surround the tray on all sides and between each item. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.workspaceButtons` | `null or boolean` | Whether workspace buttons should be shown or not. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.bars.*.workspaceNumbers` | `null or boolean` | Whether workspace numbers should be displayed within the workspace buttons. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors` | `submodule` | Color settings. All color classes can be specified using submodules with 'border', 'background', 'text', 'indicator' and 'childBorder' fields and RGB color hex-codes as values. See default values for the reference. Note that 'i3.config.colors.background' parameter takes a single RGB value. See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_changing_colors>. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.background` | `string` | Background color of the window. Only applications which do not cover the whole area expose the color. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.focused` | `submodule` | A window which currently has the focus. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.focusedInactive` | `submodule` | A window which is the focused one of its container, but it does not have the focus at the moment. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.placeholder` | `submodule` | Background and text color are used to draw placeholder window contents (when restoring layouts). Border and indicator are ignored. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.unfocused` | `submodule` | A window which is not focused. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.colors.urgent` | `submodule` | A window which has its urgency hint activated. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.defaultWorkspace` | `null or string` | The default workspace to show when i3 is launched. This must to correspond to the value of the keybinding of the default workspace. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating` | `submodule` | Floating window settings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating.border` | `signed integer` | Floating windows border width. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating.criteria` | `list of attribute set of (string or boolean)` | List of criteria for windows that should be opened in a floating mode. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating.modifier` | `string` | Modifier key or keys that can be used to drag floating windows. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.floating.titlebar` | `boolean` | Whether to show floating window titlebars. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.focus` | `submodule` | Focus related settings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.focus.followMouse` | `boolean` | Whether focus should follow the mouse. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.focus.forceWrapping` | `boolean` | Whether to force focus wrapping in tabbed or stacked containers. This option is deprecated, use {option}`focus.wrapping` instead. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.focus.mouseWarping` | `boolean` | Whether mouse cursor should be warped to the center of the window when switching focus to a window on a different output. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.focus.newWindow` | `one of "smart", "urgent", "focus", "none"` | This option modifies focus behavior on new window activation. See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#focus_on_window_activation> |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.focus.wrapping` | `one of "yes", "no", "force", "workspace"` | Whether the window focus commands automatically wrap around the edge of containers. See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_focus_wrapping> |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.fonts` | `(list of string) or (submodule)` | Font configuration for window titles, nagbar... |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps` | `null or (submodule)` | Gaps related settings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.bottom` | `null or signed integer` | Bottom gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.horizontal` | `null or signed integer` | Horizontal gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.inner` | `null or signed integer` | Inner gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.left` | `null or signed integer` | Left gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.outer` | `null or signed integer` | Outer gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.right` | `null or signed integer` | Right gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.smartBorders` | `one of "on", "off", "no_gaps"` | This option controls whether to disable container borders on workspace with a single container. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.smartGaps` | `boolean or one of "on", "off", "inverse_outer"` | This option controls whether to disable all gaps (outer and inner) on workspace with a single container. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.top` | `null or signed integer` | Top gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.gaps.vertical` | `null or signed integer` | Vertical gaps value. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.keybindings` | `attribute set of (null or string)` | An attribute set that assigns a key press to an action using a key symbol. See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#keybindings>. Consider to use `lib.mkOptionDefault` function to extend or override default keybindings instead of specifying all of them from scratch. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.keycodebindings` | `attribute set of (null or string)` | An attribute set that assigns keypress to an action using key code. See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#keybindings>. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.menu` | `string` | Default launcher to use. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.modes` | `attribute set of attribute set of string` | An attribute set that defines binding modes and keybindings inside them Only basic keybinding is supported (bindsym keycomb action), for more advanced setup use 'i3.extraConfig'. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.modifier` | `one of "Shift", "Control", "Mod1", "Mod2", "Mod3", "Mod4", "Mod5" or string` | Modifier key that is used for all default keybindings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.startup` | `list of (submodule)` | Commands that should be executed at startup. See <https://i3wm.org/docs/userguide.html#_automatically_starting_applications_on_i3_startup>. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.startup.*.always` | `boolean` | Whether to run command on each i3 restart. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.startup.*.command` | `string` | Command that will be executed on startup. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.startup.*.notification` | `boolean` | Whether to enable startup-notification support for the command. See {option}`--no-startup-id` option description in the i3 user guide. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.startup.*.workspace` | `null or string` | Launch application on a particular workspace. DEPRECATED: Use [](#opt-xsession.windowManager.i3.config.assigns) instead. See <https://github.com/nix-community/home-manager/issues/265>. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.terminal` | `string` | Default terminal to run. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.window` | `submodule` | Window titlebar and border settings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.window.border` | `signed integer` | Window border width. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.window.commands` | `list of (submodule)` | List of commands that should be executed on specific windows. See {option}`for_window` i3wm option documentation. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.window.commands.*.command` | `string` | i3wm command to execute. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.window.commands.*.criteria` | `attribute set of (string or boolean)` | Criteria of the windows on which command should be executed. A value of `true` is equivalent to using an empty criteria (which is different from an empty string criteria). |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.window.hideEdgeBorders` | `one of "none", "vertical", "horizontal", "both", "smart"` | Hide window borders adjacent to the screen edges. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.window.titlebar` | `boolean` | Whether to show window titlebars. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.workspaceAutoBackAndForth` | `boolean` | Assume you are on workspace "1: www" and switch to "2: IM" using mod+2 because somebody sent you a message. You don’t need to remember where you came from now, you can just press $mod+2 again to switch back to "1: www". |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.workspaceLayout` | `one of "default", "stacking", "tabbed"` | The mode in which new containers on workspace level will start. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.workspaceOutputAssign` | `list of (submodule)` | Assign workspaces to outputs. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.workspaceOutputAssign.*.output` | `string or list of string` | Name(s) of the output(s) from {command}`  i3-msg -t get_outputs`. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.config.workspaceOutputAssign.*.workspace` | `string` | Name of the workspace to assign. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable i3 window manager. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration lines to add to ~/.config/i3/config. |
| `xsession.windowManager.i3.package` | `null or package` | The i3 package to use. |
| `xsession.windowManager.spectrwm.bindings` | `attribute set of string` | Spectrwm keybindings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.spectrwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Spectrwm window manager. |
| `xsession.windowManager.spectrwm.package` | `package` | The spectrwm package to use. providing the spectrwm command |
| `xsession.windowManager.spectrwm.programs` | `attribute set of string` | Spectrwm programs variables. |
| `xsession.windowManager.spectrwm.quirks` | `attribute set of string` | Spectrwm quicks (custom window rules). |
| `xsession.windowManager.spectrwm.settings` | `attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string)` | Spectrwm settings. |
| `xsession.windowManager.spectrwm.unbindings` | `list of string` | List of keybindings to disable from default Spectrwm configuration. |
| `xsession.windowManager.xmonad.buildScript` | `null or absolute path` | Build script for your xmonad configuration. |
| `xsession.windowManager.xmonad.config` | `null or absolute path` | The configuration file to be used for xmonad. This must be an absolute path or `null` in which case {file}`~/.xmonad/xmonad.hs` will not be managed by Home Manager. If this option is set to a non-`null` value, recompilation of xmonad outside of Home Manager (e.g. via {command}`xmonad --recompile`) will fail. |
| `xsession.windowManager.xmonad.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable xmonad window manager. |
| `xsession.windowManager.xmonad.enableContribAndExtras` | `boolean` | Enable xmonad-{contrib,extras} in xmonad. |
| `xsession.windowManager.xmonad.extraPackages` | `unspecified value` | Extra packages available to GHC when rebuilding xmonad. The value must be a function which receives the attribute set defined in {var}`haskellPackages` as the sole argument. |
| `xsession.windowManager.xmonad.haskellPackages` | `unspecified value` | The {var}`haskellPackages` used to build xmonad and other packages. This can be used to change the GHC version used to build xmonad and the packages listed in {var}`extraPackages`. |
| `xsession.windowManager.xmonad.libFiles` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Additional files that will be saved in {file}`~/.xmonad/lib/` and included in the configuration build. The keys are the file names while the values are paths to the contents of the files. |
